feature: End-to-end four-mode integration test on fixture project
description: tests/e2e/four-modes.test.ts exercises all four modes on a single fixture project. Walks through: (1) autopilot greenfield build, (2) careful-flow refactor with disk-mechanism approvals, (3) introduce a synthetic bug, (4) diagnose mode investigates it via mocked observability MCP, (5) deployer executes a fix with checkpoint gate, (6) rollback path exercised in a follow-up, (7) postmortem auto-generated and validated. This is the spec's correctness gate — if the test passes, the vision is functional.

successCriteria.description: tests/e2e/four-modes.test.ts exists. Uses a tmpdir for the fixture project, scaffolds it from a tests/fixtures/four-modes-baseline/ directory. Cleans up after each test.
successCriteria.verificationMethod: Read test file. Verify tmpdir setup + teardown.

successCriteria.description: Autopilot phase: invokes `bober plan` + `bober run` on a small feature in the fixture. Verifies sprints execute, commits land, eval strategies pass, no orchestrator errors.
successCriteria.verificationMethod: Test passes. Verify git log of fixture shows expected sprint commits.

successCriteria.description: Careful-flow phase: switches mode='careful' and mechanism='disk', invokes `bober run` on a second feature. Test scaffolding polls .bober/approvals/*.pending.json and writes .approved.json files programmatically. Verifies audit log contains approval records.

Bug introduction: test deterministically introduces a synthetic bug into the fixture (e.g., a code change that causes a mocked-metric value to exceed threshold). The bug is committed so the diagnose phase has something to investigate.

Diagnose phase: invokes `bober incident start` with the symptom corresponding to the synthetic bug. Mocked observability MCP returns deterministic metric values. Test scaffolding auto-approves risky-action gates via disk mechanism. Diagnoser arrives at the correct root cause. Deployer executes the fix.

Resolution verification: mocked observability MCP returns post-fix metric values within threshold for the configured window. verifyResolution returns verified=true. Status transitions to 'resolved'. Postmortem auto-generates.

Postmortem validation: parse generated postmortem.md, assert all required sections present, every claim has an inline citation marker, the timeline reflects executed actions, action items are populated.

Rollback path: a follow-up sub-test invokes `bober rollback <incidentId>` on the resolved incident's changes. Verifies each rollback step goes through the gate, ChangeEntry statuses update to 'rolled-back'.
Test runs in CI (.github/workflows/ — extend existing CI workflow to include this test if it's not in the default suite). Total test runtime under 5 minutes.
successCriteria.verificationMethod: CI config includes the test. Test runtime measured <5min.

expectedChanges.description: Stdio MCP server returning deterministic metric/log responses

evaluatorNotes:
This is the integration test — verify it ACTUALLY EXERCISES the components, not just mocks them away.

Verify the mock observability MCP is REAL (a separate process speaking MCP protocol over stdio). If the test mocks at the JavaScript-function level instead of via an actual subprocess, it's not testing the Sprint 16 MCP plugin slot architecture. The integration value is testing the protocol boundary.

Verify the test uses the actual disk-approval mechanism (Sprint 9) — not a mocked mechanism. The polling, the file writes, the cleanup all need to happen for real.

Verify the test asserts on artifact existence AND content. Just checking 'file exists' is weak — assert the postmortem has at least 3 evidence citations, the audit log has entries for every gate, the changelog has matching inverses for every action.

Verify the rollback sub-test is INDEPENDENT enough that running it in isolation works (e.g., not depending on test-global state from the diagnose phase). Test ordering matters; document.

Verify CI runtime stays under 5 minutes. If the first run takes 8 minutes, identify the slow path (likely model latency if subagents are spawned with real LLM calls). Consider mocking the LLM at the spawn-mechanism level for this test specifically — the test is about orchestrator behavior, not about specific model outputs. A test-mode flag like 'BOBER_TEST_DETERMINISTIC=true' that returns canned responses would be appropriate; document if added.

Final check: the test must FAIL CLEARLY when any sprint output is broken. Run a sanity sabotage: remove `bober approve` CLI command from src/cli/, run the test, verify it fails with a meaningful error (not a cryptic timeout). Good integration tests fail loudly.
